title: LIMIT changes head of sorted result set
description:
The head of the result set returned by the following query on the http://dbpedia.org/sparql endpoint (Virtuoso 7.20) changes unexpectedly if the LIMIT clause is removed.
prefix dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
prefix rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

select ?a ?l {

    ?a a dbo:Automobile

    optional { ?a rdfs:label ?l filter ( lang(?l) = 'en') }

} order by asc(?l) limit 10

The LIMIT-less version seems to be correct, as it lists first resources with unbound labels, as per http://www.w3.org/TR/sparql11-query/#modOrderBy.
Testing with different combinations of asc/desc and limit/limitless gives the impression that the presence of limit somehow inverts the required ordering.
Works as expected if the OPTIONAL clause is removed as:
    ?a a dbo:Automobile; rdfs:label ?l filter ( lang(?l) = 'en')
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE automobiles (id UInt32, model String, year UInt32, label String);
INSERT INTO automobiles (id, model, year, label) VALUES (1, 'Model A', 2020, NULL);
INSERT INTO automobiles (id, model, year, label) VALUES (2, 'Model B', 2021, 'Car B');
SELECT model, label FROM automobiles WHERE year > 2019 ORDER BY label ASC LIMIT 5;
SELECT model, label FROM automobiles WHERE year > 2019 ORDER BY label ASC;
key_bug_features: LIMIT clause changes ordering of sorted result set
